question:
Will Russia capture Myrnohrad by November 15?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the intersection between Vatutina Vulytsya and Puhachova Vulytsya located in Myrnohrad by November 15, 2025, at 11:59 PM ET.

The intersection station will be considered captured if any part of the intersection is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by November 15, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the intersection, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Intersection Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/myrnohrad+intersection+zoom.png

Intersection Location in Myrnohrad: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/myrnohrad+intersection.jpeg

Myrnohrad Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/myrnohrad.jpeg

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/qCLFQNiDKN2ABe5Q9

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

evidences.title: ISW analyses situation around Pokrovsk and Myrnohrad
evidences.content: Долучайтеся до клубу УП! # ISW analyses situation around Pokrovsk and Myrnohrad ISW analyses situation around Pokrovsk and Myrnohrad Analysts at the Institute for the Study of War (ISW) have noted that the situation near the cities of Pokrovsk and Myrnohrad in Donetsk Oblast remains difficult, as Ukrainian forces are fighting to hold the flanks of a so-called pocket, while Russian forces continue their offensive in the area. Source: ISW Details: Ukrainian forces have reportedly liberated Rodynske, located north of Pokrovsk, as part of efforts to secure the northern flank of the pocket. Ukrainian troops are also launching counterattacks inside Pokrovsk and on its western outskirts to stop further Russian advances on the southern flank of the pocket. [...] Quote from ISW: "Russian advances in northern and western Pokrovsk have slowed in recent days, likely due to continued Ukrainian efforts to counterattack." Details: Meanwhile, Russian troops are pushing forward in the eastern and southern areas of the city, possibly trying to form a smaller pocket to help encircle Ukrainian forces in Pokrovsk and eventually force their withdrawal from the larger area. Russian forces are also bel...
evidences.query: Myrnohrad Donetsk offensive analysis Institute for the Study of War Reuters expert analysis

evidences.title: Myrnohrad - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Since the onset of Russia's offensive to reach Pokrovsk in July 2024, their forces have advanced increasingly closer to Myrnohrad; on 13 August, a Ukrainian soldier said that Russian forces in the area were tasked with the city's capture, and on 16 August, Ukrainian officials reported that Russia was six kilometers from the city. The recent advances led the population to be ordered to evacuate, with an estimated 20,000 people remaining in the city on 16 August, and a complete evacuation expected by the end of August 2024. On 19 August, Head of the Myrnohrad Military Administration Yuriy Tretyak warned that only one to three days are left to evacuate the city in face of advancing Russian troops, and that Myrnohrad was shelled for several days already. [...] Myrnohrad (Ukrainian: Мирноград, pronounced (//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:IPA/Ukrainian "Help:IPA/Ukrainian") ⓘ-Gzhegozh-%D0%9C%D0%B8%D1%80%D0%BD%D0%BE%D0%B3%D1%80%D0%B0%D0%B4.wav "File:LL-Q8798 (ukr)-Gzhegozh-Мирноград.wav"); Russian: Мирноград, romanized: Mirnograd), formerly Dymytrov until 2016, is a city in Pokrovsk Raion, Donetsk Oblast, eastern Ukraine. It was a city of regional significance "City of regional significanc...

evidences.title: Russian forces expand logistics south of Pokrovsk — ISW / The New Voice of Ukraine
evidences.content: poster Exclusive Nation business life opinion Russian war Podcasts & Video Satellite image of the residential area of ​​Pokrovsk, Donetsk Oblast, November 3, 2025 (Photo: Vantor/Handout via REUTERS) Russian forces continued advancing near the city of Pokrovsk and for the first time expanded their logistics and transport routes south of the city, the Institute for the Study of War reported on Nov. 7. Analysts examined published geolocated footage indicating that Russian forces have advanced on the northeastern and eastern outskirts of Pokrovsk, as well as in the south and north of Myrnohrad. Read also: Zelenskyy: About 300 Russian troops remain in Pokrovsk, goal No. 1 is to seize the city fast [...] Russian troops also reportedly moved forward near Krasnyi Lyman, north of Myrnohrad. ISW Photo: ISW Russian military bloggers claimed advances on the outskirts of Pokrovsk, the capture of the settlements of Rih (east of the city) and Rodynske (to the north), and further movement near Krasnyi Lyman and Rivne (east of Pokrovsk). Ad Reports also indicate attempts by Russian forces to enter southern Pokrovsk using trucks and motorcycles to deliver supplies — the first known instance of us...

evidences.title: Over 1,300 civilians left Donetsk region in one week | Donetsk Regional State Administration
evidences.content: The progress of the evacuation in Donetsk region was discussed during a regular briefing at the regional state administration with the participation of representatives of the Department of Civil Protection, Mobilization and Defense, the Service for Children's Affairs, and the Department of Social Protection of the Population. According to Dmytro Petlin from the Department of Civil Protection, Mobilization and Defense, 1,200 people remain in Pokrovsk – evacuation from there is only possible with the help of the military. About 1,000 residents remain in Dobropillia, about 900 people in Myrnohrad, and 4,800 civilians in Kostyantynivka. [...] Most people now live in Kramatorsk, Sloviansk, and Druzhkivka. It is to these settlements that people who have decided to leave the dangerous communities are being evacuated within the region. There are currently 13,213 children remaining in the Donetsk region. This was reported by Yulia Ryzhakova, head of the Children's Services Department. There are no minors left in the active combat zone. There are 547 children remaining in two settlements where families with children are being forcibly evacuated. One family with three children remains in t...

evidences.title: Fighting for Pokrovsk and Myrnohrad — DeepState reports buildup of Russian infantry and equipment / The New Voice of Ukraine
evidences.content: poster Exclusive Nation business life opinion Russian war DeepState reports Russian advances near Pokrovsk and Myrnohrad in Donetsk Oblast (Photo: DeepState) Russian forces are accumulating infantry and equipment in the southern part of Myrnohrad in Donetsk Oblast in an effort to completely capture the city and its outskirts, while also advancing toward the village of Hryshyne west of Pokrovsk, the Ukrainian monitoring project DeepState reported on April 15. According to the analysts, Russia is concentrating troops and towed artillery in southern Myrnohrad to exert pressure on the northern outskirts and fully occupy the agglomeration. The enemy controls a 20-kilometer zone from the city center and strikes “at anything that moves,” which complicates any activity in the area. [...] Will you help tell Ukraine’s story to the world? Twenty years ago, most people hadn’t even heard of Ukraine. Today, the country is on everyone’s lips and everyone’s headlines. War pushed us on the front page. But there are many other things we do that we are proud of – from music and culture to technology. We need your help to tell the world Ukrainian story of resilience, joy, and survival. If you’re wi...

In the Oleksandrivka Hromada, a car was damaged. In Andriivka, a garage was damaged. In Staroraiske of the Druzhkivka Hromada, 1 person was killed. Bakhmut Raion. In Siversk, 6 houses were damaged. evidences.title: Zelenskiy says Ukrainian forces inflict heavy losses on Russia in counteroffensive
evidences.content: Russia's Defence Ministry said its forces had seized two more localities -- Muravka, southwest of Pokrovsk, and Novoivanivka, further southwest in the Zaporizhzhia region. The general staff of Ukraine's military listed Muravka as one of several settlements where its forces had halted 87 attacks near Pokrovsk. A senior official in the Russia-appointed administration in areas of the Donetsk region held by Moscow told Russia's TASS news agency that Russian forces now had effective control of all roads and other logistics around Pokrovsk. Reuters could not independently verify battlefield reports from either side. evidences.title: School Students from Donetsk Region Worthily Represented the Region During the First Week of the National Finals of the “Side by Side All-Ukrainian School Leagues” 2025/2026 Season | Donetsk Regional State Administration
evidences.content: | Mon | Вт | Sr | Чт | Fri | Sun | Nd | --- --- --- | | | | | | | Mon | Вт | Sr | Чт | Fri | Sun | Nd | --- --- --- | | | | | | # School Students from Donetsk Region Worthily Represented the Region During the First Week of the National Finals of the “Side by Side All-Ukrainian School Leagues” 2025/2026 Season This was reported by Head of the Donetsk Regional State Administration, Head of the Regional Military Administration Vadym Filashkin. Our children competed in four sports: cheerleading, grassroots football, athletics, and orienteering. Donetsk region was represented by teams from Myrnohrad, Pokrovsk, Ocheretyne, and Velyka Novosilka. [...] The team from Pokrovsk Gymnasium No. 2 advanced to the Round of 16 in grassroots football among students in grades 1–4. The other teams also demonstrated determination, discipline, teamwork, and a strong desire to compete until the very end. “This time, there were no prize-winning places. But for children from a frontline region, participation in a national final is already a major achievement. It is valuable experience, new friendships, strong emotions, and motivation to keep moving forward. I thank the President of Ukraine, the Ministry...

evidences.title: Ukraine war latest updates: Ukraine says it halted Russian push north of Pokrovsk (7)
evidences.content: Sign up for our Newsletters→ About Team Jobs Advertising Store KI logo War # Ukraine war latest: Ukraine says it halted Russian advance north of Pokrovsk • 6 min read PreferKyiv Independent Logoon Google Avatar by Jared Goyette Ukraine war latest: Ukraine says it halted Russian advance north of Pokrovsk A soldier walks through the city of Pokrovsk in Donetsk Oblast on April 8, 2025. (Kostiantyn Liberov/Libkos/Getty Images) Hello, this is Jared Goyette from a sunny, brisk fall day in Kyiv on day 1,349 of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. [...] We are closely monitoring the situation in Pokrovsk, with reports of further deterioration in the city for Ukraine's embattled forces. The latest news is positive — a report from the Ukrainian military that the Russian advance north of the city has been halted — but the situation inside the city and to the south remains unclear. ## Ukraine says it halted Russian push north of Pokrovsk Ukrainian forces said on Nov. 3 that they stopped a Russian attempt to advance north of Pokrovsk, a key logistics hub in Donetsk Oblast, which is semi-circled by Russian forces and the site of fierce fighting.
